message:
chore(artifacts): refresh conformance/cve_arena/runtime_math/release snapshots

Regenerated tracked test artifacts to absorb the cumulative ABI growth
since the last refresh (commit 2a332ef5):

tests/conformance/*.v1.json + replacement_profile.json + stub_census.json
  - support_matrix_maintenance_report, callthrough_census,
    closure_sweep_report, conformance_coverage_snapshot,
    cross_report_consistency, fixture_pipeline, fixture_unit_tests,
    fuzz_harness_architecture, fuzz_membrane_validation,
    fuzz_phase1_targets, fuzz_phase2_targets, per_symbol_fixture_tests,
    perf_regression_prevention, replacement_zero_unapproved_fixtures,
    reverse_round_contracts, runtime_env_inventory,
    stub_todo_debt_census all rolled forward to capture the larger
    exported-symbol surface from the openat2 + statmount/listmount +
    finit_module + quotactl_fd + map_shadow_stack + bpf + kexec_load
    + kexec_file_load + NUMA mempolicy family + mseal/memfd_secret/
    rseq/cachestat + futex_waitv + mq_clock* + ns_sprintrr/rrf
    + ns_initparse + ns_parserr + ns_skiprr + ns_msg_getflag +
    inet_neta + the recent res_* alias work.

tests/cve_arena/results/...
  - bd-13ya, bd-146t, bd-15n.2, bd-18qq.4, bd-1f35, bd-1qy, bd-24b6,
    bd-24ug, bd-2xp3, bd-ahjd: artifact_index.json + trace.jsonl +
    per-mode stderr captures + report.json refreshed against the
    current arena run output.
  - corpus_normalization, format_string_validation,
    hardened_assertions, heap_overflow_validation,
    integer_overflow_validation, paired_mode_evidence, uaf_validation
    aggregate v1 reports refreshed.
  - bd-1f35 (pthread create/join churn) and bd-1qy (Rust mutex
    contract) stderr captures rolled forward to match the refreshed
    test-isolation harness.

tests/release/dossier_validation_report.v1.json
  - Release dossier regenerated against the current symbol surface
    (104-line diff is the validator emitting per-symbol coverage rows).

tests/runtime_math/*.v1.json
  - admission_gate_report, controller_ablation_report, and
    controller_manifest refreshed to match the latest deterministic
    runtime-math controller snapshots.

Pure artifact refresh: no source code touched. The upstream code
changes that produced these snapshots are in the preceding commits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

-        "count": 6,
+        "count": 11,

+          "abort",

-          "malloc"

+          "write"

-        "note": "Internal compatibility helpers allocate host-visible directory and scratch buffers in interpose mode."
+        "note": "Internal compatibility helpers allocate host-visible directory/scratch buffers and preserve low-level diagnostic abort/write fallbacks in interpose mode."
       },
       "io_internal_abi": {
         "count": 3,
@@ -215,12 +219,35 @@
         ],
         "note": "RPC/XDR compatibility helpers still allocate host-visible buffers in interpose mode."
       },
+      "signal_abi": {
+        "count": 8,
+        "functions": [
+          "raise",
+          "sigaction",
+          "sigaddset",
+          "sigemptyset",
+          "sigprocmask"
+        ],
+        "note": "Signal compatibility helpers still delegate selected sigset/action paths to host libc in interpose mode."
+      },
+      "stdio_abi": {
+        "count": 4,
+        "functions": [
+          "getenv",
+          "strlen"
+        ],
+        "note": "Stdio compatibility helpers retain selected host environment/string probes in interpose mode."
+      },
       "stdlib_abi": {
-        "count": 1,
+        "count": 5,
         "functions": [
-          "malloc"
+          "close",
+          "lseek",
+          "malloc",
+          "open",
+          "read"
         ],
-        "note": "One stdlib helper returns a host-freeable allocated string in interpose mode."
+        "note": "Selected stdlib helpers still use host-visible allocation and descriptor probing in interpose mode."
       },
       "string_abi": {
         "count": 10,
@@ -231,11 +258,14 @@
         "note": "strdup/strndup and GNU argz APIs use host malloc/free so caller-owned buffers pair with libc::free in interpose and test builds."
       },
       "unistd_abi": {
-        "count": 2,
+        "count": 20,
         "functions": [
-          "malloc"
+          "abort",
+          "clock_gettime",
+          "malloc",
+          "write"
         ],
-        "note": "Path/name helpers allocate host-visible strings in interpose mode."
+        "note": "Unistd helpers retain host-visible allocation and selected legacy diagnostic/time probes in interpose mode."
       },
       "wchar_abi": {
         "count": 1,
@@ -245,7 +275,7 @@
         "note": "Wide-string duplication returns a host-freeable allocation in interpose mode."
       }
     },
-    "total_call_throughs": 41,
+    "total_call_throughs": 80,
     "note": "Census tracks direct host call-through scan patterns used by scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh."
   }
 }
